28	#
29	# 3. TypeScript Tests (runs if libraries/typescript/** changed)
30	#    ├─ typescript-lint: ESLint checks

32	#    ├─ typescript-knip: Unused code/deps report (warning-only — never fails CI)

34	#    ├─ typescript/inspector: Unit tests for inspector package
35	#    ├─ typescript/cli: Unit tests for CLI package

83	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
84	    outputs:
85	      python: ${{ steps.out.outputs.python }}
86	      typescript: ${{ steps.out.outputs.typescript }}

88	    steps:
89	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
90	        with:
91	          ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_call' && inputs.ref || github.sha }}
92	      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
93	        id: filter
94	        if: github.event_name != 'workflow_call'
95	        with:
96	          filters: |
97	            python:
98	              - 'libraries/python/**'
99	            typescript:
100	              - 'libraries/typescript/**'
101	            create-mcp-use-app:
102	              - 'libraries/typescript/packages/create-mcp-use-app/**'
103	      - name: Compute outputs
104	        id: out
105	        uses: actions/github-script@v7
106	        env:
107	          CI_INPUT_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.python }}
108	          CI_INPUT_TYPESCRIPT: ${{ inputs.typescript }}
109	          CI_INPUT_CREATE: ${{ inputs.create_mcp_use_app }}
110	        with:
111	          script: |
112	            const fromWfCall = context.eventName === 'workflow_call';
113	            const py = fromWfCall ? (process.env.CI_INPUT_PYTHON === 'true') : ("${{ steps.filter.outputs.python }}" === 'true');
114	            const ts = fromWfCall ? (process.env.CI_INPUT_TYPESCRIPT === 'true') : ("${{ steps.filter.outputs.typescript }}" === 'true');
115	            const create = fromWfCall ? (process.env.CI_INPUT_CREATE === 'true') : ("${{ steps.filter.outputs.create-mcp-use-app }}" === 'true');
116	            core.setOutput('python', String(py));
117	            core.setOutput('typescript', String(ts));
118	            core.setOutput('create-mcp-use-app', String(create));

253	  python-transport-tests-report:
254	    needs: python-transport-tests
255	    if: always() && github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.python-transport-tests.result != 'skipped'
256	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
257	    permissions:
258	      pull-requests: write
259	    steps:
260	      - name: Download all test results
261	        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
262	        with:
263	          pattern: transport-test-results-*
264	          path: test-results
265	          merge-multiple: false
266	      - name: Generate PR comment
267	        uses: actions/github-script@v7
268	        with:
269	          script: |
270	            const fs = require('fs');
271	            const path = require('path');
272	            
273	            const transports = ['stdio', 'sse', 'streamable_http'];
274	            let tableRows = [];
275	            let allPassed = true;
276	            
277	            for (const transport of transports) {
278	              const xmlPath = path.join('test-results', `transport-test-results-${transport}`, `test-results-${transport}.xml`);
279	              let status = '⏭️ Skipped';
280	              let tests = '-';
281	              let passed = '-';
282	              let failed = '-';
283	              let errors = '-';
284	              let time = '-';
285	              
286	              if (fs.existsSync(xmlPath)) {
287	                const content = fs.readFileSync(xmlPath, 'utf8');
288	                
289	                // Parse basic stats from JUnit XML
290	                const testsMatch = content.match(/tests="(\d+)"/);
291	                const failuresMatch = content.match(/failures="(\d+)"/);
292	                const errorsMatch = content.match(/errors="(\d+)"/);
293	                const timeMatch = content.match(/time="([\d.]+)"/);
294	                
295	                if (testsMatch) {
296	                  const totalTests = parseInt(testsMatch[1]);
297	                  const failureCount = failuresMatch ? parseInt(failuresMatch[1]) : 0;
298	                  const errorCount = errorsMatch ? parseInt(errorsMatch[1]) : 0;
299	                  const passedCount = totalTests - failureCount - errorCount;
300	                  
301	                  tests = totalTests.toString();
302	                  passed = passedCount.toString();
303	                  failed = failureCount.toString();
304	                  errors = errorCount.toString();
305	                  time = timeMatch ? `${parseFloat(timeMatch[1]).toFixed(2)}s` : '-';
306	                  
307	                  if (failureCount === 0 && errorCount === 0) {
308	                    status = '✅ Passed';
309	                  } else {
310	                    status = '❌ Failed';
311	                    allPassed = false;
312	                  }
313	                }
314	              }
315	              
316	              tableRows.push(`| ${transport} | ${status} | ${tests} | ${passed} | ${failed} | ${errors} | ${time} |`);
317	            }
318	            
319	            const overallStatus = allPassed ? '✅ All transport tests passed!' : '❌ Some transport tests failed';
320	            
321	            const comment = `## Python Transport Tests Results
322	            
323	            ${overallStatus}
324	            
325	            | Transport | Status | Tests | Passed | Failed | Errors | Time |
326	            |-----------|--------|-------|--------|--------|--------|------|
327	            ${tableRows.join('\n')}
328	            `;
329	            
330	            // Find existing comment
331	            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
332	              owner: context.repo.owner,
333	              repo: context.repo.repo,
334	              issue_number: context.issue.number,
335	            });
336	            
337	            const botComment = comments.find(c => 
338	              c.user.type === 'Bot' && 
339	              c.body.includes('## Python Transport Tests Results')
340	            );
341	            
342	            if (botComment) {
343	              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
344	                owner: context.repo.owner,
345	                repo: context.repo.repo,
346	                comment_id: botComment.id,
347	                body: comment,
348	              });
349	            } else {
350	              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
351	                owner: context.repo.owner,
352	                repo: context.repo.repo,
353	                issue_number: context.issue.number,
354	                body: comment,
355	              });
356	            }
